mechanism_narrative: Higher retail sales reflect stronger consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and lifts equity prices. The relationship is monotonic over the sample period.
failure_condition: Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS level and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is ≤0 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 7% signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer income and spending, weighing on corporate profits and stock prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: More than 50% of triggered instances produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint stance of high policy rates and elevated inflation represents aggressive monetary tightening that compresses valuation multiples and depresses short-term equity returns. The interaction amplifies the negative effect beyond each factor alone.
failure_condition: In a regression of 5-day returns on FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL, and their interaction, the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at the 5% level over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts increase during periods of very low interest rates as borrowing costs fall, stimulating construction activity and broad economic growth, which supports equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: Average 60-day S&P 500 return following HOUST releases when FEDFUNDS < 1% is not significantly different from the unconditional average (two-sample t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and signals a healthy economy, leading to positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank information coefficient between INDPRO level and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is ≤0 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall sharply, it signals a downturn in the housing sector that depresses construction employment, durable goods spending, and consumer confidence, leading to negative stock market returns over the following months.
failure_condition: More than 40% of triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in employment and retail sales indicate broad-based economic strength. The joint signal of labor market health and consumer spending is a more reliable predictor of near-term equity gains than either series alone.
failure_condition: In a linear regression of 20-day returns on PAYEMS and RSAFS levels, the coefficient on their interaction is not significantly positive (p>0.10) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Very low inflation levels (close to deflation) historically precede monetary easing and can be associated with weak demand, but in the post-GFC era extremely low inflation has been accompanied by easy financial conditions, which are positive for equities. The regime of low inflation thus flips the usual negative inflation signal.
failure_condition: Average 5-day S&P 500 return when CPIAUCSL < 225 is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05) or the direction is opposite in out-of-sample periods before 2008.
